projects.title: Attention Is All You Need
projects.description: Transformer paper deep-read — academic blueprint style with structure diagrams, tables, formulas, and animation export
projects.desc: Paper-reading deck for Vaswani et al. 2017, Attention Is All You Need. Combines blueprint-style Transformer diagrams, native editable tables and formula slides, per-slide speaker notes, and narrated PPTX export.

slides.title: cover
slides.desc: Attention Is All You Need

slides.title: why it matters
slides.desc: A simpler model, better results, less training

slides.title: sequence evolution
slides.desc: From recurrent chains to attention mesh

slides.title: rnn cnn limits
slides.desc: Sequential bottleneck, path length, memory ceiling

slides.title: architecture overview
slides.desc: Figure 1 Transformer architecture

slides.title: encoder decoder
slides.desc: Encoder stack vs decoder stack

slides.title: scaled dot-product
slides.desc: The attention equation

slides.title: multi-head attention
slides.desc: Eight parallel attention heads

slides.title: three uses
slides.desc: Encoder self, decoder masked, cross attention

slides.title: ffn residual layernorm
slides.desc: The supporting layer components

slides.title: positional encoding
slides.desc: Sinusoidal order injection

slides.title: complexity table
slides.desc: Why constant path length matters

slides.title: training setup
slides.desc: Hardware, schedule, optimizer, regularization

slides.title: results
slides.desc: WMT14 BLEU and training cost

slides.title: ablations
slides.desc: Five takeaways from the variation study

slides.title: conclusion
slides.desc: One paper, a generation of models

projects.title: LoRA Hu 2021
projects.description: LoRA paper reading — blueprint technical style with structure diagrams, tables, KPI cards
projects.desc: Technical reading deck for Hu et al. 2021, LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models. Combines blueprint-style AI diagrams, native editable charts and tables, per-slide speaker notes, and a narrated PPTX export.

slides.title: cover
slides.desc: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

slides.title: agenda
slides.desc: What this talk covers

slides.title: problem
slides.desc: Full fine-tuning is infeasible at scale

slides.title: limitations
slides.desc: Why existing efficient methods fall short

slides.title: low-rank insight
slides.desc: Weight updates are intrinsically low-rank

slides.title: method
slides.desc: Freeze W0, inject low-rank BA

slides.title: implementation
slides.desc: One alpha constant, one deployment merge

slides.title: transformer
slides.desc: Adapting attention weights only

slides.title: advantages
slides.desc: Four key advantages of LoRA

slides.title: latency
slides.desc: Adapters add latency; LoRA does not

slides.title: setup
slides.desc: Covering NLU to NLG across four model types

slides.title: glue
slides.desc: Fewer params, equal or better results

slides.title: gpt-3
slides.desc: Holds at 175B scale

slides.title: understanding
slides.desc: Which to adapt? How large a rank?

slides.title: conclusion
slides.desc: Conclusion and impact
